text.title: Agent Rules for Statistical Analysis
text.summary: Rules for agents running or reviewing A/B test significance calculations, including common LLM failure modes.

rule.statement: Always require raw counts (n1, x1, n2, x2) before computing significance; refuse to calculate from headline percentages alone.
rule.rationale: LLMs confidently compute p-values from percentages without knowing sample sizes, producing meaningless results. Raw counts are the only defensible input.

rule.statement: Pre-register primary metric, sample size, and end date BEFORE launch; refuse to analyze a test where these were not fixed in advance.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc metric selection and peeking invalidate p-values. Tests without pre-registration are exploratory at best — report them as such.

rule.statement: Always report 95% CI alongside p-value; output a binary significant_at_0.05 field rather than reporting "almost significant" (p=0.06).
rule.rationale: "Almost significant" is not a statistical concept. p=0.06 is not significant at alpha=0.05. CI gives the range of plausible effects — report both.

rule.statement: Apply Bonferroni correction (alpha/k) when checking more than 3 metrics; designate one primary metric and treat the rest as guardrails.
rule.rationale: With 10 independent metrics at alpha=0.05, expected false positives = 0.5 per test. Bonferroni correction divides alpha by the number of comparisons.

section.title: When to Escalate

list.item: Asymmetric traffic split (90/10): use statsmodels, not the simplified formula
Revenue / AOV metric: use bootstrapping (10k resamples), not proportions z-test
0 conversions in one variant: use Fisher's exact test; z-test breaks
Multi-armed or sequential design: use SPRT or Bayesian — standard z-test inflates FPR

section.reference: Python helper for z-test + power analysis using statsmodels

antipattern.description: Reporting "95% chance B is better" from a frequentist p-value.
antipattern.reason: That interpretation is Bayesian. Frequentist: "If H0 is true, there is a 5% chance of seeing this data." Report CI instead: "We are 95% confident the true lift is between X% and Y%."

antipattern.description: Silently dropping alpha and power assumptions from sample-size output.
antipattern.reason: Sample size is meaningless without the alpha and power used to compute it. Always include: alpha=0.05, power=0.80 (or whatever was used) in the output.
